failedAssertions.reason: Backend WebSocket handler (QuerySocketHandler.send_query_update in app.py) calls QueryResults(uuid, future) without get_values=True. The 'values' field is never included in the WS response. ResultsPanel.tsx requires 'values' in result objects to render the table ('values' in (val as object) filter on line ~295). Results always show 'No results found' despite backend executing queries correctly (74-148 rows confirmed by direct WS test). Fix: pass get_values=True in QuerySocketHandler, or update ResultsPanel to not require 'values' for filter check.
Blocked by same root cause as VAL-EXEC-005: results table never renders, so symbol selector cannot appear.
Blocked by same root cause as VAL-EXEC-005: results table never renders, so column sort cannot be tested.
Blocked by same root cause as VAL-EXEC-005: results table never renders, so pagination cannot be tested.
Blocked by same root cause as VAL-EXEC-005: results table never renders, so CSV download button is never shown.

Cancel button is visible during running state (pass), but clicking Cancel shows 'WebSocketError' instead of 'Query cancelled.' Root cause: cancelQuery() calls ws.close() which triggers ws.onerror asynchronously. ws.onerror lacks a cancellation guard and overwrites executionStatus from 'cancelled' to 'error'. Fix: add isCancellingRef flag in QueryContext.tsx and check it in ws.onerror to skip the error transition when cancelling.

rootCauses.description: Backend/frontend 'values' field mismatch in WebSocket query results. QuerySocketHandler.send_query_update() in app.py calls QueryResults(self.uuid, future) without get_values=True. responses.py QueryResults only includes 'values' when get_values=True. ResultsPanel.tsx filters results using "'values' in (val as object)" — this always fails, resultsMap is null, and 'No results found' is always shown.
rootCauses.suggestedFix: In app.py QuerySocketHandler.send_query_update(), change QueryResults(self.uuid, future) to QueryResults(self.uuid, future, get_values=True). Alternatively, update ResultsPanel.tsx to check for 'columns' instead of 'values' as the existence check, and only require 'values' when rendering rows.

rootCauses.description: Cancel query race condition: cancelQuery() calls ws.close() which triggers ws.onerror asynchronously. ws.onerror lacks a cancellation guard and overwrites executionStatus from 'cancelled' to 'error', showing a WebSocket error message instead of 'Query cancelled.'
rootCauses.suggestedFix: In QueryContext.tsx (or wherever ws event handlers are defined), add an isCancellingRef = useRef(false) flag. Set it to true in cancelQuery() before calling ws.close(). In ws.onerror, check if (isCancellingRef.current) return; to skip the error transition during cancellation.
